text: The very rare verb which our Lord uses here probably means to ‘stammer’, to ‘hesitate’. The heathens used to address their gods by a series of titles, with the superstitious idea that the prayer would not be heard unless the right title was hit upon.
‘Daily’: the Latin here (but not in Lk. 11.3) coins the wordsupersubstantialis, which has sometimes been understood as a direct reference to the Holy Eucharist.
‘Height’; the Greek word here used can also mean ‘length of life’; and some have thought that ‘cubit’ is used, by a metaphor, for a short space of time.
